cards.title: Risk Classification
cards.content:
ΓÇó Standard Lives: Normal health, charged normal premium.
ΓÇó Sub-Standard Lives: Higher risk (due to health/habits), charged Extra Premium.
ΓÇó Declined Lives: Risk is too high to insure.

cards.title: Hazards
cards.content:
ΓÇó Physical Hazard: Relates to health, occupation (e.g., mining), or dangerous hobbies.
ΓÇó Moral Hazard: Intentional acts, fraud, or hidden motives (e.g., buying huge insurance with no income).

items.text: Medical Underwriting: Based on doctor's reports, ECG, blood tests.
Non-Medical: Approved solely based on the proposal form declarations (usually for young age/low sum assured).
Financial Underwriting: Ensuring the Sum Assured matches the person's income (HLV).

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Numerical Rating System: Assigning positive/negative points to various health factors to decide the final premium.
Lien: A temporary reduction in the death benefit for a specific period (used for sub-standard lives instead of extra premium).
Female insurance sometimes requires special underwriting regarding pregnancy risks.
The Agent is the "Primary Underwriter" because they see the prospect face-to-face.
Occupational Hazard: Jobs like circus acrobats or explosives handlers attract higher premiums.
